name: Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Gallocanta offers routes across a high continental plain situated at over 1,000 meters in altitude. The terrain is generally flat and open, characterized by a steppe-like environment interspersed with extensive cereal fields. The region's most prominent natural feature is the Laguna de Gallocanta, a large saltwater lagoon. The Santa Cruz Mountain Range also provides varied scenery for some routes. Best mountain bike trails around Gallocanta The most popular mountain bike trail is Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular, an 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, bordering the protected area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01), a moderate 36.5 miles (58.7 km) path that guides cyclists around the lake and past various observatories. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Barrueco – Castillo de Berrueco loop from Tornos, a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail leading through hills and open fields, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Gallocanta is defined by a high continental plain, a large saltwater lagoon, and a steppe-like environment. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Gallocanta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gallocanta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Gallocanta?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails in the Gallocanta area, offering a variety of routes for different preferences. Most of these routes are rated as moderate, with a few easier options available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Gallocanta?
A: The Gallocanta region features a high continental plain over 1,000 meters in altitude, characterized by generally flat and open steppe-like terrain and extensive cereal fields. Trails primarily consist of unpaved roads and wide paths, making them accessible for easy to moderate ability levels. Some routes, particularly those venturing towards the Santa Cruz Mountain Range, may include varied scenery and occasional steep slopes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around the Laguna de Gallocanta?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available that skirt the Laguna de Gallocanta, allowing cyclists to enjoy panoramic views and pass by bird observatories. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.1-mile (29.1 km) trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999042462/reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-desde-tornos-btt01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 36.5-mile (58.7 km) path.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Gallocanta?
A: Gallocanta is particularly notable for its birdlife, especially thousands of common cranes that stop over between November and February. While mountain biking is possible year-round, visiting outside of the peak crane migration season might offer a quieter experience on the trails. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for cycling.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gallocanta?
A: The Gallocanta area is well-suited for mountain biking with its generally flat and open terrain, consisting mostly of unpaved roads and wide paths. Many routes are accessible for easy and moderate ability levels, making them suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.1-mile (29.1 km) route that borders the protected area and offers scenic views.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Gallocanta?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the Laguna de Gallocanta, you can encounter various points of interest. Many routes pass by bird observatories, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement&lt;/a&gt; near the lagoon. The trails also traverse a steppe-like environment with extensive cereal fields and offer views of the Santa Cruz Mountain Range.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes in the broader Gallocanta area offer views of historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24545154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barrueco – Castillo de Berrueco loop from Tornos&lt;/a&gt; is a 13.7-mile (22.1 km) trail that leads through hills and open fields, providing glimpses of the Castillo de Berrueco. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Santed – Puerto de Balconchán loop from Santed&lt;/a&gt;, which passes by the Castillo de Santed.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Gallocanta?
A: The Gallocanta area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Mountain bikers often praise the unique steppe-like environment, the panoramic views of the Laguna de Gallocanta, and the accessibility of the trails, which are primarily unpaved roads and wide paths suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999042462/reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-desde-tornos-btt01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.5-mile (58.7 km) path that guides cyclists around the lake. Another substantial route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon – Laguna de Gallocanta loop from Tornos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 45.1 km (28 miles).

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on Gallocanta mountain bike trails?
A: Given the region's high continental plain and generally flat terrain, many trails have relatively modest elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984502109/reserva-natural-dirigida-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 75 meters. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999042462/reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-gallocanta-circular-desde-tornos-btt01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Tornos (BTT01)&lt;/a&gt; can have more significant climbs, around 460 meters, as they cover more ground and may venture into areas with varied topography.

Q: Are there any specific safety considerations for mountain biking in Gallocanta?
A: While the terrain is generally accessible, some routes may feature steep slopes. Care is advised on downhill stretches, where farm vehicle ruts can be present. It's always recommended to check current trail conditions and weather forecasts before heading out. The area's cycling routes are typically well-signed with dedicated MTB signs and information panels.
tips: Beautiful hermitage in a spectacular setting, such as the Gallocanta lagoon. One of the best places to watch birds. There are informative panels about the lagoon and the birds, a place to make a barbecue and an area to take refuge in case of a storm.

From this observatory we will have privileged views of the Gallocanta Lagoon from the heights, it is recommended to go with binoculars to see the birds up close. The trails that surround the lagoon are ideal for gravel or mountain biking.

The Gallocanta lagoon is the largest saltwater lagoon in Europe. A spectacular natural environment to enjoy nature and the popular cranes (there are viewpoints from where we can observe them with binoculars)
The colors that it gives us at sunset are beautiful.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement (Gallocanta Lagoon)
